heading: Adjustment for current policies

children.text: payments made under (referred to in this section as “Payment for Physicians’ Services”);section 1395w–4 of title 42
the Estate and Gift Tax under subtitle B of title 26;
the AMT; and

children.label: A
children.text: the provisions of those 2 Acts that were made permanent by the Pension Protection Act of 2006 ();Public Law 109–280

children.label: B
children.text: amendments to the Estate and Gift Tax referred to in paragraph (2);

children.label: C
children.text: the AMT referred to in paragraph (3); and

children.label: D
children.text: the income tax rates on ordinary income that apply to individuals with adjusted gross incomes greater than $200,000 for a single filer and $250,000 for joint filers.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: b Duration
children.text: This section shall remain in effect through .December 31, 2011

children.heading: Criteria
children.label: 1 Criteria
children.text: Legislation that includes provisions amending or superseding the system for updating payments under subsections (d) and (f) of shall trigger the current policy adjustment required by this chapter.section 1395w–4 of title 42

children.label: A
children.text: estimated net outlays attributable to the payment rates and related parameters in accordance with subsections (d) and (f) of (as scheduled on , to be in effect); andsection 1395w–4 of title 42December 31, 2009

children.label: i
children.text: the nominal payment rates and related parameters in effect for 2009 had been in effect through , without change; andDecember 31, 2014

children.label: ii
children.text: thereafter, the nominal payment rates and related parameters described in subparagraph (A) had applied and the assumption described in clause (i) had never applied.

children.label: A
children.text: estimated net outlays attributable to the payment rates and related parameters specified in (as scheduled on , to be in effect for the period of time covered by the relevant provisions of the eligible legislation); andsection 1395w–4 of title 42December 31, 2009

children.label: B
children.text: what those net outlays would have been if the nominal payment rates and related parameters in effect for 2009 had been in effect, without change, for the same period of time covered by the relevant provisions of the eligible legislation as under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Criteria
children.label: 1 Criteria
children.text: Legislation that includes provisions amending the Estate and Gift Tax under subtitle B of title 26 shall trigger the current policy adjustment required by this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: total revenues projected to be collected under title 26 (as scheduled on , to be in effect); andDecember 31, 2009

children.label: B
children.text: what those revenue collections would have been if, on the date of enactment of the legislation meeting the criteria in paragraph (1), estate and gift tax law had instead been amended so that the tax rates, nominal exemption amounts, and related parameters in effect for tax year 2009 had remained in effect through , with nominal exemption amounts indexed for inflation after 2009 consistent with subsection (g).December 31, 2011

children.label: A
children.text: total revenues projected to be collected under title 26 (as scheduled on , to be in effect for the period of time covered by the relevant provisions of the eligible legislation); andDecember 31, 2009

children.label: B
children.text: what those revenues would have been if the estate and gift tax law rates, nominal exemption amounts, and related parameters in effect for 2009, with nominal exemption amounts indexed for inflation after 2009 consistent with subsection (g), had been in effect for the same period of time covered by the relevant provisions of the eligible legislation as under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Duration of policy adjustment
children.label: 4 Duration of policy adjustment
children.text: Adjustments made pursuant to this subsection are available for policies affecting the estate and gift tax through only . Any adjustments shall include budgetary effects in all years from these policy changes.December 31, 2011

children.heading: Criteria
children.label: 1 Criteria
children.text: Legislation that includes provisions extending AMT relief shall trigger the current policy adjustment required by this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: total revenues projected to be collected under title 26 (as scheduled on , to be in effect); andDecember 31, 2009

children.label: B
children.text: what those revenue collections would have been if, on the date of enactment of legislation meeting the criteria in paragraph (1), AMT law had instead been amended by making commensurate adjustments in the exemption amounts for joint and single filers in such a manner that the number of taxpayers with AMT liability or lost credits that occur as a result of the AMT would not be estimated to exceed the number of taxpayers affected by the AMT in tax year 2008 in any year for which relief is provided, through .December 31, 2011

children.label: A
children.text: total revenues projected to be collected under title 26 (as scheduled on , to be in effect for the period of time covered by the relevant provisions of the eligible legislation); andDecember 31, 2009

children.label: B
children.text: what those revenues would have been if, on the date of enactment of legislation meeting the criteria in paragraph (1), AMT law had instead been amended by making commensurate adjustments in the exemption amounts for joint and single filers in such a manner that the number of taxpayers with AMT liability or lost credits that occur as a result of the AMT would not be estimated to exceed the number of AMT taxpayers in tax year 2008 for the same period of time covered by the relevant provisions of the eligible legislation as under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Duration of policy adjustment
children.label: 4 Duration of policy adjustment
children.text: Adjustments made pursuant to this subsection are available for policies affecting the AMT through only . Any adjustments shall include budgetary effects in all years from these policy changes.December 31, 2011

children.heading: Permanent extension of middle-class tax cuts
children.label: f Permanent extension of middle-class tax cuts
A
children.text: The 10 percent bracket as in effect for tax year 2010, as provided for under section 101(a) of EGTRRA and any later amendments through .December 31, 2009

children.label: B
children.text: The child tax credit as in effect for tax year 2010, as provided for under section 201 of EGTRRA and any later amendments through .December 31, 2009

children.label: C
children.text: Tax benefits for married couples as in effect for tax year 2010, as provided for under title III of EGTRRA and any later amendments through .December 31, 2009

children.label: D
children.text: The adoption credit as in effect in tax year 2010, as provided for under section 202 of EGTRRA and any later amendments through .December 31, 2009

children.label: E
children.text: The dependent care credit as in effect in tax year 2010, as provided for under section 204 of EGTRRA and any later amendments through .December 31, 2009

children.label: F
children.text: The employer-provided child care credit as in effect in tax year 2010, as provided for under section 205 of EGTRRA and any later amendments through .December 31, 2009

children.label: G
children.text: The education tax benefits as in effect in tax year 2010, as provided for under title IV of EGTRRA and any later amendments through .December 31, 2009

children.label: H
children.text: The 25 and 28 percent brackets as in effect for tax year 2010, as provided for under section 101(a) of EGTRRA and any later amendments through .December 31, 2009

children.label: I
children.text: The 33 percent bracket as in effect for tax year 2010, as provided for under section 101(a) of EGTRRA and any later amendment through , affecting taxpayers with adjusted gross income of $200,000 or less for single filers and $250,000 or less for joint filers in tax year 2010, with these income levels indexed for inflation in each subsequent year consistent with subsection (g).December 31, 2009

children.label: J
children.text: The rates on income derived from capital gains and qualified dividends as in effect for tax year 2010, as provided for under sections 301 and 302 of JGTRRA and any later amendment through , affecting taxpayers with adjusted gross income of $200,000 or less for single filers and $250,000 for joint filers with these income levels indexed for inflation in each subsequent year consistent with subsection (g).December 31, 2009

children.label: K
children.text: The phaseout of personal exemptions and the overall limitation on itemized deductions as in effect for tax year 2010, as provided for under sections 102 and 103 of EGTRRA of 2001, respectively, and any later amendment through , affecting taxpayer with adjusted gross income of $200,000 or less for single filers and $250,000 for joint filers, with these income levels indexed for inflation in each subsequent year consistent with subsection (g).December 31, 20091So in original. Probably should be “taxpayers”.1

children.label: L
children.text: The increase in the limitations on expensing depreciable business assets for small businesses under as in effect in tax year 2010, as provided under section 202 of JGTRRA and any later amendment through .section 179(b) of title 26December 31, 2009

children.label: A
children.text: total revenues projected to be collected and outlays to be paid under title 26 (as scheduled on , to be in effect); andDecember 31, 2009

children.label: B
children.text: what those revenue collections and outlay payments would have been if, on the date of enactment of legislation meeting the criteria in paragraph (1), the provisions identified in paragraph (1) were made permanent.

children.label: A
children.text: total revenues projected to be collected and outlays to be paid under title 26 (as scheduled on , to be in effect for the period of time covered by the relevant provisions of the eligible legislation); andDecember 31, 2009

children.label: B
children.text: what those revenue collections and outlay payments would have been if, on the date of enactment of legislation meeting the criteria in paragraph (1), the provisions identified in paragraph (1) had been in effect, without change, for the same period of time covered by the relevant provisions of the eligible legislation as under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Indexing for inflation
children.label: g Indexing for inflation
children.text: Indexed amounts are assumed to increase in each year by an amount equal to the cost-of-living adjustment determined under for the calendar year in which the taxable year begins, determined by substituting “calendar year 2008” for “calendar year 1992” in subparagraph (B) of such section.section 1(f)(3) of title 26

children.heading: Guidance on estimates and current policy adjustments
children.label: h Guidance on estimates and current policy adjustments
A
children.text: each of the income tax provisions shall be estimated as though the AMT had remained at current law as scheduled on to be in effect; andDecember 31, 2009

children.label: B
children.text: if more than 1 of the income tax provisions is included in a single piece of legislation, those provisions shall be estimated in the order in which they appear.2So in original. Probably should be “are”.2

children.heading: AMT
children.label: 2 AMT
children.text: For purposes of estimates made pursuant to subsection (e), changes to the AMT shall be estimated as if, on the date of enactment of legislation meeting the criteria in subsection (e)(1), all of the income tax provisions identified in subsection (f)(1) were made permanent.
